text: Unit tests check individual parts of software, like a single function. Integration tests make sure those parts work together correctly. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write the test first, then code to pass it. This approach helps prevent bugs early. Using both unit and integration tests gives developers confidence to change code, as they know the tests will catch mistakes. This is especially useful when working on large projects or in teams. Overall, good testing is not just about finding problems—it is about building reliable software and reducing stress for developers.
